subject: capybaras
one_liner: Programmable messaging that arrives whenever it arrives.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan telecom that treats unhurriedness as a paid feature. Capybaras are the calmest animal alive; they do not rush, do not startle, and share one hot spring with everyone. Lull. is a serious communications API built around that. SLAs measured in vibes, delivery 'eventually', one phone number for the whole colony. Pitch it like a well-funded developer platform that genuinely believes latency is a personality flaw. Never wink. Confident, dry, faux-enterprise.

hero.h1: The messaging API that refuses to hurry.
hero.sub: SMS, voice, and push for animals who have never once checked their phone. Your message goes out when the colony is ready. Usually it is not.

features.title: Unhurried Delivery
features.blurb: Outbound messages queue behind whatever the capybara is currently doing, which is nothing. Two delivery tiers: 'soon' and 'when it gets there.' We do not offer 'now.' Now is a human problem.

features.title: One Shared Colony Number
features.blurb: Every capybara on your account answers the same phone number. Private channels were considered and rejected. If one of them gets a text, all of them get the text. This is the product working correctly.

features.title: Co-Regulation Webhooks
features.blurb: No message fires until the herd collectively agrees it is a fine time. Each outbound event ships with a matching calm signal so nobody is ever startled. Your 2FA code arrives once everyone has settled.

pricing_tiers.name: Wading
pricing_tiers.tagline: For one capybara and a low-stakes pond.
perks: Up to 12 messages a month, give or take

pricing_tiers.name: Fully Submerged
pricing_tiers.tagline: For a serious colony with real communication needs it will ignore.
perks: Unmetered messages, none of them urgent
Webhook that fires after a nap

pricing_tiers.name: Apex Chill
pricing_tiers.tagline: Enterprise serenity for fleets of more than forty capybaras.
perks: A named contact who will get back to you in a few days
We will not call you. You will not call us.

testimonials.quote: We migrated our entire alerting stack to Lull. and our incident response time went from four minutes to roughly a long weekend. Honestly, the alerts were stressing everyone out.
testimonials.name: Devon Marsh
testimonials.title: Head of Reliability, a wetland co-working space for rodents

testimonials.quote: I sent a password reset on Tuesday. It arrived. I no longer remember what it was for, but it arrived, and that is the SLA they promised.
testimonials.name: Priya Otterton
testimonials.title: Solo Founder & Sole User, CapyChat

testimonials.quote: The shared colony number means I receive every message intended for forty-one other capybaras. I have never felt more included in a communications platform.
testimonials.name: Big Gary
testimonials.title: Verified Capybara, Tier 2 Soaker

demo_screen_concept: A 'Serenity Console' messaging dashboard. Left rail: channel toggles (SMS / Voice / Push) shown as puffy clay switches and a shared colony inbox count. Center: an outbound message queue where each message drifts through deadpan statuses ('soaking', 'considering', 'drifted off', 'delivered (eventually)') driven by a slow client-side timer; a composer to send a test message that meanders through those statuses; a status filter. Right rail: a live 'Colony Vibe' meter (1-10, pinned low and calm), a 'Nudge gently' button that visibly accomplishes almost nothing, and a Calm/Chaos slider that, when pushed toward Chaos, just snaps itself back to Calm. All mock data, all vanilla JS.

design_direction.style: Claymorphism onsen — warm bathhouse clay. Soft, chunky, toy-like UI on a steamed-cream ground: puffy panels at 18-24px radius, 3px clay borders, and layered inner+outer shadows so every surface looks pressed from warm terracotta putty. One terracotta carries structure and CTAs; an onsen-water teal marks calm and status signals. Gentle squish-on-press, slow float, no hard edges, no neon, no gradient soup. Reads like a children's-toy SDK shipped by a telecom that has fully given up on urgency.
design_direction.archetype: Developer / communications API product landing (programmable messaging platform), rendered as soft tactile clay instead of the usual dark technical theme
design_direction.motion_vocab: Slow and unbothered: 250ms ease-out transitions, soft squish on press via cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1), a gentle 6s float on hero clay blobs, status pills that ease between states rather than snap. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Centered hero on steamed-cream ground with two soft out-of-focus 'steam' blobs floating behind. A puffy clay product card lockup with the Lull. wordmark, the refuses-to-hurry headline, sub, and the primary CTA as a chunky terracotta clay button beside a secondary teal-outlined 'open the demo' button. A small clay 'status: soaking' pill sits in the corner as proof of concept.
